title: Case Studies from the Field
author: Treelands Foundation
excerpt: Real-world lessons from our conservation and agroforestry projects across Karnataka — what works, what doesn't, and why community involvement is the key.
body: Across our projects in Mysuru and Shivamogga, we have learned that lasting forest conservation begins with the people who live closest to the forest. This collection of field case studies documents the practical realities of restoring degraded landscapes.
From selecting the right native species to building local nurseries, each case study captures the decisions, setbacks and successes that shape sustainable natural resource management.
We share these openly so that other practitioners, students and communities can learn from our experience and adapt it to their own landscapes.

title: Training Volunteers to Educate
author: Treelands Foundation
excerpt: How we build a network of informed volunteers who carry forward the message of tree planting and conservation in their own communities.
body: Awareness is the first seed of change. Our public education programmes train volunteers — students, farmers and citizens — to become local champions for trees and biodiversity.
Through hands-on workshops, volunteers learn species selection, planting techniques, aftercare and the ecological role each tree plays.
Equipped with this knowledge, they educate their neighbours and schools, multiplying impact far beyond what any single organisation could achieve.

title: Latest Reports from the KA Forest Department
author: Treelands Foundation
excerpt: A round-up of recent reports and developments from the Karnataka Forest Department and what they mean for forestry in the state.
body: Staying informed about state forestry policy helps us align our projects with the wider conservation agenda. Here we summarise recent reports and notifications from the Karnataka Forest Department.
We highlight the implications for agroforestry, medicinal plants and community forestry, and how landowners and entrepreneurs can respond.
Our consultancy team is always available to help interpret these developments for your specific landscape or project.
